content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` is the placeholder "TODO" and `NSCameraUsageDescription` ("This app needs camera access.") is generic and does not name the document-scanning feature (Info.plist:9–13).
Pierre: Apple rejects apps whose purpose strings are empty, placeholder, or too vague to tell the user why data is needed. Here the location string is literally "TODO" (an unshipped placeholder) and the camera string is a generic "needs access" line even though ScannerView.swift shows a clear document-scanning feature. Rewrite the camera string to reference scanning documents (e.g. "SnapScan uses the camera to scan and capture your documents") and either supply a real location purpose tied to a visible feature or remove the key if location isn't actually used.
